vocab.example: As a child, having a blank canvas allowed me to unleash my imagination and create worlds of my own.

vocab.meaning: kỹ năng vận động tinh (sự khéo léo của đôi tay)
vocab.example: Learning to handle a delicate paintbrush at a young age really helped develop my fine motor skills.

vocab.meaning: phương tiện nghệ thuật (chất liệu vẽ)
vocab.example: Watercolors were my preferred artistic medium back then because of the way the colors blended so fluidly.

vocab.meaning: sự phối hợp giữa tay và mắt
vocab.example: Sketching from life was a rigorous exercise that significantly enhanced my hand-eye coordination.

vocab.meaning: sự thể hiện bằng hình ảnh
vocab.example: I struggled with words, so drawing became my primary mode of visual representation for my feelings.
I used to doodle aimlessly in the margins of my school notebooks whenever I felt bored during class.
My art teacher introduced us to basic color theory, teaching us how to mix primary colors to create secondary hues.
I wasn't much for realism; I preferred abstract expression where I could just play with shapes and splatters.

vocab.meaning: vẽ quan sát (vẽ lại những gì nhìn thấy thực tế)
vocab.example: We spent many afternoons doing observational drawing in the school garden to capture the intricate details of nature.

vocab.meaning: khả năng cảm thụ thẩm mỹ
vocab.example: Early exposure to painting nurtured my aesthetic sensibility, making me more appreciative of beauty in everyday life.
